does: This paper documents Scania's implementation of ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, focusing on how the company structured adoption through team-based onboarding programs and established safety guardrails. The work describes practical deployment strategies that improved productivity metrics, quality outputs, and innovation outcomes across diverse operational functions. Rather than a traditional research contribution, this represents a real-world case study of large-scale enterprise AI integration, detailing the organizational mechanisms—governance frameworks, training approaches, and policy safeguards—that enabled widespread adoption while maintaining control over model behavior and output reliability.
connects: This work builds on emerging research in organizational AI adoption and responsible AI deployment at scale. It responds to prior challenges in enterprise AI integration documented in studies of technology diffusion, organizational change management, and AI safety in production environments. The paper connects to broader conversations about designing effective human-AI collaboration frameworks and establishing guardrails for large language models in corporate settings. It extends prior work on team-based AI training methodologies and institutional governance structures for managing AI risks, while addressing practical questions about how established manufacturers can transition legacy workforces to AI-augmented processes.
enables: This case study establishes a model for how large, geographically distributed organizations can safely scale foundation models across diverse teams and functions. It points toward future research on measuring productivity gains from AI integration, understanding organizational factors that predict successful AI adoption, and developing more sophisticated guardrail architectures that balance enablement with safety. The work enables exploration of how team-based governance structures generalize across different industry sectors and workforce compositions, and suggests pathways for studying long-term effects of widespread AI augmentation on labor, skill development, and organizational culture in manufacturing and beyond.
